insight.ko:
## Key Insight
한반도와 중동의 동시다발적 지정학적 위기가 글로벌 자산 시장의 뇌관을 건드리고 있습니다. 한국은 '전쟁 추경'이라는 초유의 사태로 원화 자산 패닉 셀링이 발생한 반면, 글로벌 시장은 이란발 원유 공급 차질 우려와 강달러 현상에 촉각을 곤두세우는 모습입니다.

## Market Overview
한국 증시는 대통령의 '전쟁 추경' 시정연설 소식에 안보 불안이 극대화되며 코스피와 코스닥 모두 4% 넘게 폭락했습니다. 원/달러 환율 역시 1,520원 선을 돌파하며 외국인 자본 이탈과 극단적인 위험 회피 심리를 반영하고 있습니다. 

미국 증시는 전일 기준 스페이스X의 기업공개(IPO) 추진 등 기술주 호재에 힘입어 상승 마감했습니다. 하지만 트럼프 전 대통령의 이란 타격 경고 이후 중동 긴장이 고조되고 있어, 최신 데이터 미반영 상태인 미국 시장도 향후 변동성 확대가 불가피해 보입니다.

## Cross-Market Signals
- **유가 급등 + 달러 강세 → 글로벌 인플레이션 재점화 우려**: 중동 리스크로 WTI 유가가 104달러를 돌파하고 달러 인덱스가 상승하면서, 에너지 수입국의 물가 상승 압력이 다시 커질 것으로 보입니다.
- **한국 증시 폭락 + 원/달러 환율 급등 → 코리아 리스크 프리미엄 최고조**: 한반도 안보 위기가 실물 경제의 자본 이탈로 직결되며, 원화 자산에 대한 전면적인 재평가가 이루어지고 있습니다.
- **금 하락 + 암호화폐 약세 → 극단적 불확실성 속 현금 선호**: 전통적 안전자산인 금과 대체자산인 비트코인이 동반 하락하는 것은, 투자자들이 포지션을 청산하고 기축통화인 달러 현금 확보에 나서고 있음을 시사합니다.
insight.en:
## Today's Key Insight

South Korean markets are buckling under the dual weight of acute domestic security anxieties and escalating Middle Eastern tensions. As the Korean administration pushes a highly unusual "war supplementary budget" through the National Assembly, the resulting capital flight is severely punishing local equities and the currency. Compounding this regional stress is a looming global energy shock, with oil prices surging past $104 a barrel following explicit US threats of military action against Iran.

## Market Overview

**Korean Markets**
The KOSPI and KOSDAQ suffered massive sell-offs today, plunging over 4% as domestic political rhetoric regarding a "war budget" triggered aggressive risk aversion. This severe equity contraction, paired with the Won depreciating sharply past the 1,520 mark against the Dollar, suggests a rapid withdrawal of foreign capital as geopolitical risk premiums are aggressively priced into Korean assets.

**US Markets**
Based on the previous session's close (April 1), Wall Street exhibited tech-led resilience, with the Nasdaq advancing over 1% and the S&P 500 posting solid gains. However, these figures do not reflect today's escalating geopolitical developments; the impending SpaceX IPO and historic NASA launches provided a bullish narrative yesterday that will likely be tested by today's crude oil spike and shifting global risk sentiment.

## Cross-Market Signals

- **WTI Oil Surge + Geopolitical Threats:** Crude oil jumping nearly 5% past $104 directly correlates with US threats to strike Iran, signaling a potential supply disruption that threatens to inject a fresh inflationary shock into the global economy.
- **USD/KRW Breakout + KOSPI Plunge:** The Won's steep depreciation alongside a 4% equity market collapse indicates acute capital flight from South Korea, likely driven by institutional alarm over the government's emergency defense spending measures.
- **Crypto Contraction + Dollar Strength:** Declines in Bitcoin and Ethereum, coupled with a rising Dollar Index, suggest a broader liquidation of speculative, high-beta assets as investors pivot toward traditional fiat liquidity havens amid rising global friction.

en.title: Donations received directly into YouTubers' accounts must also be reported to the National Tax Service
en.summary: Media content creators, such as YouTubers, must report details of individual donations received in their accounts to the National Tax Service starting in April. The National Tax Service announced on the 2nd that it has added 'media content creation business' to the list of industries required to prepare cash receipt statements, starting with the preliminary value-added tax return in April, to improve the tax transparency of YouTubers and others. Accordingly, if YouTubers receive individual donations directly into their accounts from viewers, they must submit the channel name, account number, and amount received. Failure to submit will result in a penalty tax of 1% of the unsubmitted amount. Also, starting with this report, the penalty tax imposed when a business operator issues or receives a tax invoice without supplying goods or services has been raised from 3% to 4%. The number of corporations subject to the preliminary VAT return in April is 672,000, an increase of about 22,000 compared to the same period last year. Corporations subject to reporting must report and pay the value-added tax on their business performance from January 1 to March 31 of this year by the 27th. A total of 225 including individual general taxpayers (2.07 million) and small corporations (182,000)

pool.title: Ukraine war briefing: Russia responds to Zelenskyy’s Easter truce offer with drone attack
pool.summary: Ukrainian president says ceasefire could show diplomacy works, while Russia dismisses statement as ‘PR stunt’. What we know on day 1,499Volodymyr Zelenskyy has criticised Russia for responding to an offer of an Easter truce with airstrikes. The Ukrainian president said on Wednesday he had spoken to US negotiators about an Easter ceasefire but Russian forces had fired more than 700 drones – many of them Iranian-designedShaheds– targeting parts of western and central Ukraine in a rare daytime attack. Zelenskyy said: “Russia is responding [to the Easter ceasefire offer] with Shahed drones and continues its terrorist operations against our energy sector, against our infrastructure,” adding that he had discussed ways of advancing diplomacy with US negotiators. “A silence over Easter could be exactly the signal that tells everyone that diplomacy can be successful.” Russia’s foreign ministry rejected Zelenskyy’s proposal as a “PR stunt”.The Ukraine president said talks with US mediators aimed at resolving the four-year conflict were “positive”. The talks were held remotely on Wednesday with the US special envoy, Steve Witkoff, Donald Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner and the US senator Lindsey Graham, with Nato’s secretary general, Mark Rutte, also joining the call amidthe alliance’s continuing tensionswith Washington. Zelenskyy thanked the US for its efforts to bring about peace and said the Ukrainian and US teams had agreed to strengthen a document outlining US security guarantees for any future peace deal. “This is precisely what could pave the way for a reliable end to the war.” In recent weeks Zelenskyy said the US had been pressuring Ukraine to make concessions to bring a quick end to the conflict after the US and Israel launched the war on Iran in late February. Talks with Russia are deadlocked over the question of land, with Ukraine refusing to cede to Moscow’s demands that it relinquish the eastern region of Donbas.Russia claimed to have full control of Ukraine’s Luhansk region on Wednesday, which Kyiv denied.Russia’s defence ministry claimed its forces had taken control of the entire Luhansk region – part of the Donbas – but a Ukrainian military official said small areas were still held by Ukrainian forces. Russia has previously made false claims of advances. The Russian defence ministry said in a statement: “Units have completed the liberation of the Luhansk people’s republic.” But Viktor Tregubov, a spokesperson for Ukrainian forces, said there were no changes to report in that region. “Unfortunately, we only hold small patches [in Luhansk], but those positions have been held by 3rd brigade for a long time,” Trehubov told the Associated Press. Russian claims of progress have in the past proved to be inaccurate. The Moscow-appointed head of Luhansk announced its full capture last June. Ukrainian officials have said that Moscow makes false claims of advances to persuade US negotiators a Russian victory in Ukraine is inevitable.Russia fired hundreds of drones at Ukraine, killing at least five people and destroying a postal terminal, Ukrainian officials have said. Ukraine’s Nova Poshta mailing company published an image on Wednesday of a warehouse in the western city of Lutsk in flames, with thick smoke pouring from its roof. As well firing 339 drones at Ukraine overnight, Russia launched more than 360 drones during the day, the Ukrainian air force said. One drone killed four people in the central Cherkasy region, while an earlier drone strike on a car in Ukraine’s frontline Kherson region killed a woman and badly wounded two other people, regional authorities said.Continue reading...
